name: Holo Linux
summary: A real riscv64 Linux, verified and booted in a browser tab.
comment_shared: Holo Linux vendors xterm.js + addons under ./vendor and ships no OS-shell chrome, so the whole app lives in one service-worker scope and boots offline/standalone. Nothing is pulled from the shared OS surface at runtime.

description.p: A real Linux 6.6 kernel boots a Debian 13 (trixie) userland on the holospaces RISC-V core — entirely in the tab, no install, no server, no VM manager.

The kernel and rootfs are content-addressed. Before a single guest instruction runs, Holo Linux re-derives their SHA-256 κ and refuses to boot on any mismatch (Law L5). The kernel's κ is the same content address pinned canonically at /boot/kernel.uor.json.

ul: A real riscv64 Linux 6.6 kernel, not a scripted shell
A genuine Debian rootfs — bash, coreutils, cc, apt
κ-gated boot: bytes admitted by content address, not trust
A persistent, writable ext4 on a sparse κ-disk
Verify it yourself: uname, /proc, ps, compile + run C

releases.description: κ-gated boot of a real riscv64 Linux 6.6 + Debian 13 userland, with an in-browser self-verification panel.
